sourceMovie.title: The Fountain
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Fountain for the same reason it broke through in 2006: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Darren Aronofsky keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mr. Nobody
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Fountain clicked for you, Mr. Nobody (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Vesper
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Fountain clicked for you, Vesper (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Fountain?
faq.answer: Try Mr. Nobody, Vesper, Meet Joe Black—each shares DNA with The Fountain while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Fountain?
faq.answer: The Fountain (2006) is best described as Drama, Adventure, Science Fiction, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Fountain worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
